methodology: Ranked by recency-weighted category wins (decay=0.95/week). Most recent week counts full value; each prior week counts 5% less. At 25 weeks, week 1 still counts ~29% of current — history matters, but teams playing well right now get the edge. Cumulative AP wins used as tiebreaker.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: One week in and Duke Joe already looks like a problem. 75 category wins and a perfect 9-0 all-play record is the kind of opening week that does not happen by accident. Balanced across every category: offense, pitching, saves, speed. Nobody in this league came within 17 cat wins of them. If this is the floor, everyone else is playing for second.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: Second-best all-play record in the league after one week, and the rotation is the reason. Michael came into the season with the deepest staff in the draft and Week 1 delivered on that promise. The 8-1 all-play tells you this team was not beatable by most of the field. Only one roster out of nine had the firepower to take a head-to-head against them.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Third in all-play and Garth's offense is already clicking. Yamamoto and Skubal gave the pitching foundation it needed and the lineup did its part. The 55 cat wins put them firmly in the top tier after one week. The early concern: saves. Limited closer depth in a league where SV is a full scoring category is a problem that does not fix itself.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Right at the contender line after Week 1 despite a rotation that is missing Brown, Miller, and Strider before a single game was played. Rose has Shohei carrying the offense and 42 cat wins without much pitching contribution is actually a decent result. The IL situation is not small. The question is whether the offense can keep this ranking intact while the rotation heals.

rankings.name: Ray Donovan
rankings.analysis: Identical cat wins to Busch Latte but one fewer AP win slots Mario in fifth. The closer depth is real and the offense has Acuna Jr. waking up. What Week 1 exposed is the rotation ceiling: three reliable starters means innings and quality starts will cap out faster than any other team at this level. Solid opening week. The rotation concern is real and is not going away.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: The defending champion opens in sixth place. The 36 cat wins are middle of the pack and the 3-5-1 all-play record is not what anyone penciled in for the 2025 title holder. De La Cruz brings speed, Luzardo brings strikeouts, but the saves column is empty and the lineup cannot produce power or OBP at a level that competes with the top half of this league. One week. But it is not a good one.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: Freeman, Witt Jr., Yordan, Cruz, Devers. On paper, one of the best lineups in this draft. Week 1 produced 32 cat wins and a 2-6-1 all-play record, which does not match the roster sheet at all. TylerR has too much talent for this ranking to stick. Week 1 was a bad week from a good team. That is the most charitable read of the data, and it is probably the correct one.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: Skenes, deGrom, Flaherty, Lugo, Imanaga. That rotation should not be producing 27 cat wins in Week 1. The all-play record of 2-6-1 matches the category output and neither number is explainable given the names on the roster. Early-season rust or something actually broken: Week 2 will answer that question. Too much talent here to panic. Too bad a week to ignore.

rankings.name: The Ragans Administration
rankings.analysis: Ohtani on the mound and the team finishes 23rd in category wins. That sentence should not be possible. The pitching can get there but the offense is the problem: 23 cat wins with a legitimate ace is bottom-two production. TylerV needs the lineup to wake up or Shohei pitching is just a fun fact with no fantasy impact. Week 1 result: structurally concerning.

rankings.name: Good Vibes Only
rankings.analysis: Dead last. Burnes, Greene, and Schwellenbach all opened the season on IL, which is not a rotation problem so much as it is a rotation-does-not-exist problem. The lineup has Harper, Betts, and Rodriguez and still posted near the bottom of the league in OBP. 20 cat wins out of 90 possible. Michael has real pieces on this roster. They were not available in Week 1.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Two weeks in and already running away from the field. 75 cat wins in Week 1 was historically dominant. Week 2 cooled to a normal-person 52 cat wins, which still ranked fourth in the league. Duke Joe has built the most balanced roster in this league and there is no evidence yet that anyone can challenge it.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: Locked into #2 through two weeks. The Week 2 pitching performance was the best in the league: 1.64 ERA, 0.92 WHIP, 8 quality starts. Week 1 had them at 8-1 in all-play. Week 2 dropped to 5-4 in all-play but the actual matchup win was a blowout. Michael built a rotation that quietly dominates while the rest of the league panics about theirs.

rankings.name: Ray Donovan
rankings.analysis: The biggest mover through two weeks. Week 1 was middling, then Glasnow locked in and the entire rotation went 74 K, 2.10 ERA in Week 2, the best pitching week in the league. Mario has the deepest closer trio in the game and the offense has Acuna Jr. waking up. The only question is whether a three-starter rotation holds up longer than a few weeks.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Consistent is the right word. Rose has been right at the bubble between tier 2 and tier 3 for two straight weeks, which is exactly where a team with a depleted rotation should be. Shohei, Carroll, and Chisholm keep the offense humming. The rotation losing Brown, Miller, and Strider to IL is the cloud hanging over everything. If those three come back healthy, this ranking goes up. If they don't, it goes down.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Week 1 had Garth looking like a top-three team. Week 2 had him looking like a replacement-level roster. The talent is real: Yamamoto and Skubal is one of the better SP duos in this league, and the offense has pop. The variance between weeks is the concern. A 55 cat win week followed by a 35 cat win week means either something broke or the lineup is streakier than the draft board suggested. Watch Week 3 carefully.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: The most interesting team in this league right now. Week 1 was a 32-cat disaster. Week 2 was a 55-cat performance that was second-best in the entire league. TylerR has a legitimately elite lineup: Freeman, Witt Jr., Yordan, Cruz, Devers. The ceiling is obvious. The season record sits at 7-11-2 because the schedule has been a mugging. This ranking will keep climbing as long as the lineup stays healthy.

rankings.name: Good Vibes Only
rankings.analysis: The biggest two-week climb in the league. Week 1 was 0-9 all-play with 20 cat wins, the worst in the league by a distance. Week 2 was 5-4 all-play with 47 cat wins, genuinely middle of the pack. Crochet is elite, the lineup has Harper and Julio Rodriguez, and Michael is managing around the Burnes and Greene IL situations. The floor was exposed in Week 1. Week 2 showed the ceiling. The real team is probably somewhere in between.

rankings.name: The Ragans Administration
rankings.analysis: The trajectory is pointing in the right direction and it still isn't enough. Week 1 was a disaster (23 cat wins, 2-7 all-play). Week 2 was better (42 cat wins, 4-5 all-play). The improvement is real but the cumulative hole from Week 1 keeps TylerV pinned in the bottom third. Shohei on the mound and a deep rotation mean the pitching is the best part of this team. The offense needs to wake up or this ranking stays here.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: The biggest fall. Week 1 was respectable (3-5 all-play, 36 cat wins). Week 2 was historically bad: 0-8-1 all-play, 18 category wins out of 90. The 2025 champion is in ninth place with 54 cumulative cat wins. Ete has speed (De La Cruz) and K upside (Luzardo and the rotation), but zero saves and a lineup that cannot hit for power or get on base have made both of those upsides irrelevant. Something needs to change.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: The worst cumulative cat total in the league: 42 wins out of 180 total category decisions. Week 2 had Skenes, deGrom, Flaherty, Lugo, and Imanaga post zero quality starts with a 4.80 ERA. A rotation like that should not be doing what Rain City's rotation is doing right now. Whether it's pitch count management, early-season rust, or something actually broken, Week 3 is the verdict. Taylor needs answers before this gets away from him completely.

rankings.name: Keanu Reeves
rankings.analysis: Still #1 cumulative, but the cushion is thinner than it has been all season. 219 cat wins through 4 weeks. Week 4 was Duke Joe’s worst all-play (4–5, 39 cat wins) and the rotation gave up a 4.73 ERA. Juan Soto just hit IL-10. The lead is real money short, and Nick-fil-A is the next opponent.

rankings.name: The Buckner Boots
rankings.analysis: 194 cumulative cat wins, 22–8–6 all-play. Won 6–4 over Allahu in Week 4 with 14 HR and 7 QS. The rotation depth (Sale, Webb, Bradley, Valdez, Rogers) is overwhelming and now Adley Rutschman’s IL-10 is one of the few problems on this roster. Holding firm at #2.

rankings.name: Nick-fil-A
rankings.analysis: Up one spot to #3 after an 8–2 demolition of Good Vibes Only. 51 cat wins this week, 187 cumulative. Mason Miller (3 SV) and Ryan Helsley (2 SV) anchored the bullpen and James Wood (3 HR, 3 SB) provided the offense. Mario looks like a real contender now, not a lucky winner.

rankings.name: One Ball Two Strikes
rankings.analysis: Up one spot to #4 after upsetting Keanu 6–3 at 24% sim probability. 51 cat wins, 187 cumulative. Garth’s rotation posted a 2.67 ERA and José Ramírez stole 6 bases. Two straight strong all-play weeks (8–1 in Week 3, 5–1–3 in Week 4). The roster is finally clicking.

rankings.name: The Ragans Administration
rankings.analysis: Up two spots to #5 after the most dominant single week of the season: 9–0 all-play, 67 cat wins, 8–1 H2H beatdown of Busch. Aaron Judge and Mike Trout both went 5 HR. The rotation has Cole Ragans, Ohtani, King, Schlittler, Woodruff, and Mitch Keller fronting it. TylerV is climbing fast and a few more weeks like this puts him in the top tier.

rankings.name: Busch Latte
rankings.analysis: Down three spots to #6 after a 1–7–1 all-play disaster (29 cat wins). The pitching IL list (Strider, Brown, Miller, Imai) finally caught up to Rose at the worst possible time. Got Ragans-ed 8–1. Sal Stewart and JJ Wetherholt are still hitting but the staff is in shambles. 171 cumulative cat wins keeps Busch in the top half but the trajectory is wrong.

rankings.name: Allahu Alvarez
rankings.analysis: Down one spot to #7. 4–5 all-play in Week 4, 13–21–2 cumulative. Yordan Alvarez (3 HR, 6 RBI) and Oneil Cruz (2 HR, 4 SB) put up real offensive numbers but the staff posted a 5.18 ERA in a 4–6 loss to Buckner. Now Freddie Freeman is on the NA list and the rotation is missing Pivetta, Steele, and Henderson. TylerR’s roster is leaking value at every position.

rankings.name: Good Vibes Only
rankings.analysis: Holding at #8 only because Ete Crow and Rain City are worse, not because Michael deserves the spot. 0–9 all-play this week. Zero. 21 cat wins. 6.72 ERA. Zero saves. Mookie Betts, Brent Rooker, and Ryan Pepiot all on the IL. There is no offense, no rotation, and no closer. The season is in free fall.

rankings.name: Ete Crow
rankings.analysis: Holding at #9 despite a 5–4 all-play in Week 4 (40 cat wins). Won the H2H 6–3 over Rain City on the strength of 42 RBI (league best) and 9 HR. The 8–19–3 standings record is bad, but Jace has won or tied three of the last four weeks. The roster the league thought was done finishing 1st in 2025 is starting to look like it again.

rankings.name: Rain City Bombers
rankings.analysis: Locked in at #10. 1–5–3 all-play, 35 cat wins. The rotation posted a 2.14 ERA and 1.10 WHIP, both league-best for the second straight week. The lineup put up 30 R, 5 HR, 28 RBI. Skenes and deGrom are doing their jobs. The hitters are not. Two weeks of elite pitching wasted on the worst offense in the league.
